name: chat-output-display
title: Chat Output Display
purpose: Unified formatting for the chat-output surface — context snapshots (full box), context tickers (compact 1-line for transient transitions), cross-repo switch indicators, and status markers. Consolidates the previous `context-display` and `task-status-report` rules under one capability so all surfaces share a glyph vocabulary and emission policy. Closes the context-agreement gap between agent state and user visibility.

requirements.title: Context Snapshot Content
requirements.body: The Context Snapshot (full box) MUST display: change ID and title, success criteria count, gate progress (inline visual), task counts by status, and current workdir. The snapshot MUST fit within 10 lines for quick scanning.

scenarios.title: Snapshot includes all required fields
given: An active change with tasks, gates, and a proposal
then: The output includes the change ID and title
The output includes gate progress as an inline visual
The output includes task counts grouped by status
The output includes the current workdir path

scenarios.title: Snapshot fits within 10 lines
given: A change with 20 tasks across 4 sections and all 7 gates
then: The total output is 10 lines or fewer

requirements.title: Context Snapshot Emission Triggers
requirements.body: The Context Snapshot (full box) MUST be emitted by tools that represent major state transitions: adv_change_create, adv_gate_complete, adv_change_reenter, adv_status (primary change only — see rq-ctxticker2.4), and on session resume with an active change. adv_change_show provides structured JSON for direct LLM consumption and does NOT emit a snapshot. Transient task-state tools emit a Context Ticker instead — see rq-ctxticker2.

scenarios.title: Snapshot emitted on change creation
given: An agent creates a new change via adv_change_create
then: A context snapshot (full box) is included in the tool output

scenarios.title: Snapshot emitted on gate transition
given: A gate is marked complete via adv_gate_complete
then: The updated gate progress is visible in subsequent tool output as a full-box snapshot

scenarios.title: Snapshot emitted on gate re-entry
given: A change with completed gates
scenarios.when: adv_change_reenter reopens gates from a specified point
then: The tool output includes an updated _contextSnapshot (full box)
The snapshot gate progress shows the reset gate state

requirements.title: Context Ticker Content
requirements.body: The Context Ticker MUST be a single-line, ≤80-column compact summary with three segments separated by middle dots: change ID (truncated to ≤20 characters with `…` suffix on overflow), gate arrow (`{prev} ✓→{next}`, `release ✓` if all gates done, or `proposal ○→discovery` if none done), and task progress in `{done}/{total}` form. The ticker uses box-drawing rails (`║ ... ║`) to share visual vocabulary with the full snapshot and remain deterministic (identical input → identical output).

scenarios.title: Ticker is a single line
given: An active change with at least one task and gates partially complete
then: The output contains exactly one line
The output is no more than 80 columns wide
The output contains the box-drawing rail character ║

scenarios.title: Ticker truncates long change IDs
given: A change ID longer than 20 characters
then: The change ID segment is truncated to at most 20 characters
The truncated segment ends with the ellipsis character …

scenarios.title: Ticker shows compact gate arrow
given: A change with proposal/discovery/design done and planning pending
then: The output contains the gate arrow `design ✓→planning`
The output does not contain the full gate-progress glyph list

requirements.title: Context Ticker Emission Triggers
requirements.body: The Context Ticker MUST be emitted (instead of the full snapshot) by transient task-state tools: adv_task_update transitioning to in_progress or done, adv_task_ready, adv_task_add, and adv_task_cancel. Other emission sites (adv_change_create, adv_gate_complete, adv_change_reenter, adv_status) MUST continue to emit the full snapshot per rq-ctxsnap2.

scenarios.title: Ticker emitted by adv_task_update on in_progress / done transitions
given: An active change with a pending task
scenarios.when: adv_task_update transitions the task to in_progress or done
then: The tool output includes a single-line context ticker as _contextSnapshot
The output is not a multi-line full-box snapshot

scenarios.title: Ticker emitted by adv_task_ready
given: An active change
then: The tool output includes a single-line context ticker as _contextSnapshot

scenarios.title: Ticker emitted by adv_task_add and adv_task_cancel
given: An active change
scenarios.when: adv_task_add successfully creates a task or adv_task_cancel cancels one or more tasks
then: The tool output includes a single-line context ticker as _contextSnapshot

scenarios.title: Full-box snapshot still emitted by gate / change tools
given: An active change
scenarios.when: adv_change_create, adv_gate_complete, or adv_change_reenter is invoked
then: The tool output includes a multi-line context snapshot (full box) as _contextSnapshot

scenarios.title: adv_status emits full-box for primary change, ticker for non-primary
given: adv_status with multiple active/draft/pending changes in recentChanges
then: The first active/draft/pending change (primary) includes a multi-line full-box _contextSnapshot
Subsequent changes (non-primary) include a single-line context ticker as _contextSnapshot

requirements.title: IDLE / ATTN Status Marker Split
requirements.body: The status resolver MUST distinguish two distinct user-visible states: ATTN (user must act — permission pending, approval, or question) and IDLE (agent finished, no user action needed — session start or completed work). resolveStatus MUST return ATTN only when permissionPending is set, and IDLE when the session is idle without sub-agents or long tools. The initial status state MUST be IDLE so a fresh session is not falsely flagged as needing user attention.

scenarios.title: sessionIdle resolves to IDLE
given: Plugin state where sessionIdle is true and permissionPending is false and no sub-agents are active
then: The returned marker is IDLE

scenarios.title: permissionPending resolves to ATTN
given: Plugin state where permissionPending is true
then: The returned marker is ATTN regardless of other flags

scenarios.title: Initial state is IDLE
given: A freshly initialised plugin status state
scenarios.when: getStatus is called before any transitions
then: currentStatus is IDLE

requirements.title: IDLE Marker Constant
requirements.body: The STATUS_MARKERS enum MUST contain an IDLE entry mapping to the textual marker `[ADV:IDLE]`. The IDLE emoji MUST be the white square (⬜) — visually distinct from ATTN's red square (🟥) and color-blind safe paired with the textual marker.

scenarios.title: STATUS_MARKERS contains IDLE
given: The STATUS_MARKERS constant is loaded
then: The value is the literal string [ADV:IDLE]

scenarios.title: IDLE emoji is distinct from ATTN
given: The terminal status emoji map
scenarios.when: getStatusEmoji is called for IDLE and ATTN
then: IDLE returns ⬜
ATTN returns 🟥
The two values are not equal

requirements.title: IDLE Bell Policy
requirements.body: The terminal bell MUST ring on transitions from active work into the IDLE marker (WORK→IDLE and TOOLING→IDLE) using the same debounce/armed state machine as ATTN. The bell MUST NOT ring on IDLE→IDLE (no transition), on BLOCKED→IDLE (recovery without user action), or on lateral IDLE↔ATTN transitions (already user-visible state).

scenarios.title: WORK → IDLE rings
given: Previous status is WORK
then: The bell rings (immediately when not armed, debounced when armed)

scenarios.title: BLOCKED → IDLE does not ring
given: Previous status is BLOCKED
then: The bell does not ring
Any pending bell timer is cancelled

scenarios.title: IDLE ↔ ATTN does not double-ring
given: Status has already transitioned into IDLE or ATTN and rung the bell
scenarios.when: Status moves laterally between IDLE and ATTN
then: The bell does not ring again for the lateral transition

requirements.title: Cross-Repo Switch Indicator
requirements.body: When the agent switches workdir to a different repository during a change, a formatted indicator MUST be emitted showing the from/to paths and the task that triggered the switch. The indicator MUST be ≤3 content lines (excluding box borders) so it stays compact when emitted alongside other context surfaces. This prevents the user from losing track of which repository context the agent is operating in without flooding scrollback.

scenarios.title: Switch indicator shows from/to paths
given: A change with cross-repo tasks
The agent is switching workdir from repo A to repo B
scenarios.when: The cross-repo switch indicator is rendered
then: The output shows the source repository path
The output shows the target repository path
The output shows the task that triggered the switch

scenarios.title: Switch indicator is at most 3 content lines
given: A cross-repo switch with typical from-path, to-path, and task title
then: The rendered output has no more than 3 content lines (excluding ╔ and ╚ box borders)

requirements.title: Context Display Format Constraints
requirements.body: All context display formatting (snapshot, ticker, cross-repo switch) MUST use box-drawing characters consistent with existing ADV patterns (banner.ts). The format MUST be deterministic — identical state produces identical output. The compact ticker and the cross-repo switch indicator MUST stay within 80 columns; long content (change IDs, paths, task titles) is truncated rather than wrapped. The full Context Snapshot prioritises gate-progress visibility (rq-ctxsnap1) over the 80-column budget — its box grows naturally to fit the inline gate row, and only the CONTEXT line truncates the change ID to keep the overall budget bounded. The output MUST NOT include interactive prompts or block execution.

scenarios.title: Deterministic output
given: A change with known state (fixed tasks, gates, workdir)
scenarios.when: A context snapshot or ticker is rendered twice with the same input
then: Both outputs are identical

scenarios.title: Non-blocking display
given: A context snapshot or ticker is being rendered
then: No interactive prompts are displayed
Execution is not blocked waiting for user input

scenarios.title: Compact surfaces never exceed 80 columns
given: A long change ID and a long combined cross-repo from→to path
scenarios.when: The compact ticker or cross-repo switch indicator is rendered
then: Each emitted line is ≤80 columns wide
The full snapshot box is exempt — its width is driven by the gate-progress row (rq-ctxsnap1) and may exceed 80 cols when all 7 gates are displayed

scenarios.title: Snapshot CONTEXT line truncates long change IDs
given: A change ID longer than the available width on the CONTEXT line
then: The CONTEXT line displays the change ID truncated with an ellipsis suffix
The CONTEXT line itself stays within the snapshot's bounded width

requirements.title: Context Display Graceful Degradation
requirements.body: The context snapshot and ticker formatters MUST gracefully handle missing or partial data: absent gate info, empty task lists, missing proposal, unavailable workdir. Partial outputs are emitted with placeholder values rather than errors.

scenarios.title: Missing gates handled gracefully
given: A change with no gate data
then: Gate progress shows all gates as unknown/pending
No error is thrown

scenarios.title: Empty task list handled gracefully
given: A change with zero tasks
scenarios.when: A context snapshot or ticker is rendered
then: Task counts show 0 for all statuses
No error is thrown

requirements.title: ADV Tool Display Titles
requirements.body: ADV plugin tools SHOULD provide deterministic, concise display titles through the OpenCode plugin SDK title surfaces when those surfaces are available. The title is presentation metadata only; the tool's machine-readable response MUST remain available as the JSON `output` string when a structured ToolResult is returned.

scenarios.title: Registered ADV tool returns display title and parseable output
given: An ADV tool is registered through the plugin registry
then: The result includes a concise display title when returned through a structured ToolResult
The result output field remains a JSON-parseable string
The public tool name and argument schema are unchanged

scenarios.title: Title generation is deterministic
given: The same ADV tool name and same display-safe arguments
scenarios.when: A display title is generated twice
then: Both generated titles are identical
No ADV state, workflow query, or tool output parsing is required to compute the title

requirements.title: Tool Titles Are Display-Only Metadata
requirements.body: Tool display titles MUST NOT be used as authority for correctness, security, permissions, persistence, workflow state, gate completion, or spec compliance. Those decisions MUST continue to use structural tool names, typed arguments, schemas, state machines, validators, persisted state, and tests.

scenarios.title: Permission and workflow logic ignore titles
given: An ADV tool result includes a display title
scenarios.when: Permission, workflow, gate, or persistence logic evaluates the tool call
then: The logic uses the structural tool name, arguments, schemas, and persisted workflow state
Changing or omitting the display title does not change authorization, gate, or persistence behavior

requirements.title: Tool Title Redaction and Bounds
requirements.body: Tool display titles and display metadata MUST redact sensitive argument values and bound long or opaque values before exposing them. Sensitive keys include password, token, secret, apiKey, credential, and privateKey variants. Long display snippets MUST be truncated rather than wrapped or emitted in full.

scenarios.title: Sensitive values are redacted
given: Tool arguments include token-like, secret-like, credential-like, or password-like keys
scenarios.when: A display title or display metadata is generated
then: Sensitive values are omitted or replaced with a redaction marker
The raw sensitive value does not appear in the title or display metadata

scenarios.title: Long values are bounded
given: Tool arguments include a long command, path, query, or identifier
then: The display title remains bounded to a concise single-line label
Long snippets are truncated with an ellipsis rather than emitted in full
